cli_probes.command: ./workflow discover "handoff artifact publication consumption lineage checkpoints subscriptions evidence"
./workflow recall "handoff artifacts cqrs authority subscriptions receipts outbox run_nodes graph"
cli_probes.error_message: WORKFLOW_DATABASE_URL must be set in process env or declared in /Users/nate/Praxis/.env

cli_probes.command: ./workflow query "What are the authoritative surfaces today for handoff, receipts, outbox, system events, workflow notifications, event log, and run_nodes?"
cli_probes.error_message: WORKFLOW_DATABASE_URL must be set in process env or declared in /Users/nate/Praxis/.env

ground_truth.note: Database-backed recall and query authority were unavailable in this shell, so the matrix is grounded in source files rather than live DB memory.

matrix_legend.keep: Keep as a first-class surface. Tighten adoption, not existence.
matrix_legend.contain: Keep the surface but narrow its authority so it cannot sprawl into a competing truth source.
matrix_legend.collapse: Replace the surface with a projection or narrower seam; do not preserve it as a standalone durable authority.

current_state: The repo already has real authority seams for workflow control, receipts, compile artifacts, subscriptions, and run/job execution.
The handoff seam now exists as a durable CQRS-style publish/bind/consume surface over compile artifacts plus subscription checkpoints.
The highest architectural risk is overlapping evidence and event surfaces, not missing capability.
must_do: Finish adopting the existing handoff authority as the only sanctioned cross-step durable transfer path.
Assign one explicit truth role to each event and evidence surface so operators and future agents stop guessing.
Fence run_nodes as an internal operating-model substrate instead of letting it grow into a second workflow primitive.
should_do: Centralize system_events writes through one authority helper instead of scattering inserts across runtime modules.
Either rebuild workflow_notifications as a projection over outbox and subscriptions or prove it is purely operator UX and nothing else.
Keep graph and AI harness layers narrow: retrieval, traversal, context building, invocation, verification, and tool gating only.
remove_simplify_change: Do not add a new graph authority.
Do not add a new event bus.
Do not add a parallel artifact registry.
Do not let event_log, system_events, workflow_outbox, workflow_notifications, and receipts masquerade as the same truth source.

surface_rows.current_role: Canonical workflow run and job lifecycle lane.
surface_rows.truth_boundary: Owns workflow submission, claim, execute, complete, retry, cancel, and run-state recomputation for the main workflow model.
surface_rows.must_change: Stay the single first-class workflow lane. Do not let operating-model card execution become a peer workflow model.
surface_rows.why: This surface already exists, already owns the core execution lifecycle, and already exposes the right operational verbs.

surface_rows.current_role: Durable handoff authority for definition, plan, and packet lineage artifacts.
surface_rows.truth_boundary: Owns publish, bind, consume, reusable lookup, lineage, and stale or ambiguous reuse rejection through compile artifacts plus subscription checkpoints.
surface_rows.must_change: Standardize all durable cross-step handoff on this seam. Do not create a parallel artifact registry or ad hoc handoff blob format.
surface_rows.why: This was the real missing primitive. It now exists and should become the canonical handoff path.

surface_rows.truth_boundary: All receipt reads and writes go through the receipts table; legacy receipt index tables are not runtime authority.
surface_rows.must_change: Remain the authoritative evidence surface. Outbox, notifications, and event streams may project from it but must not replace it.
surface_rows.why: The repo already treats receipts as canonical evidence, and the implementation says so explicitly.

surface_rows.current_role: Read contract over committed workflow evidence for subscribers.
surface_rows.truth_boundary: Copies committed authority rows into a replayable run-scoped feed ordered by evidence_seq; does not mutate lifecycle truth.
surface_rows.must_change: Stay read-only and derived. Do not let transport or notification concerns leak back into lifecycle truth.
surface_rows.why: The module is already explicit that it is a narrow read contract over committed evidence.

surface_rows.current_role: Subscription definitions and acknowledgement checkpoints for consumers of committed outbox facts.
surface_rows.truth_boundary: Owns subscription compatibility, last_authority_id, and last_evidence_seq watermarks without mutating runtime lifecycle truth.
surface_rows.must_change: Keep this as the only place that advances consumer cursors for committed handoff and outbox consumption.
surface_rows.why: The repo already models checkpoints as explicit consumer acknowledgements rather than hidden queue state.

surface_rows.current_role: Control-plane lifecycle facts and compatibility events.
surface_rows.truth_boundary: Should own command and workflow lifecycle fact emission only; should not become a generic event sink or audit replacement.
allowed_to_own: workflow completion and failure lifecycle facts
surface_rows.must_change: Centralize writes through one authority emitter. Today multiple runtime modules insert system_events directly.
surface_rows.why: The surface is useful, but its write boundary is too loose and easy to confuse with broader event or evidence truth.

surface_rows.current_role: Channel-scoped event stream with cursor reads and LISTEN/NOTIFY wakeups.
surface_rows.truth_boundary: Owns stream delivery and UI or service wakeups; must not become canonical workflow state or evidence truth.
surface_rows.must_change: Use it as transport and responsiveness only. Do not use it to answer authoritative questions about workflow lifecycle or evidence.
surface_rows.why: The module itself says cursor reads are authoritative for this stream, but not for business truth outside the stream.

surface_rows.current_role: Delivered and undelivered workflow job notifications for polling consumers.
surface_rows.truth_boundary: At most this should be an operator inbox view; it should not remain a standalone durable truth source beside receipts and outbox.
surface_rows.must_change: Collapse into a projection over committed outbox facts and subscription checkpoints, or keep it only as an explicitly derived UX surface.
surface_rows.why: This is the most obvious overlap candidate: durable notifications sit beside receipts and outbox while describing the same workflow outcomes.

surface_rows.current_role: Internal card-execution substrate for operating-model flows.
surface_rows.truth_boundary: May own run-node state transitions for card execution, but must not become a second general-purpose workflow model.
allowed_to_own: card node claim and terminal transitions
surface_rows.must_change: Fence it from API, MCP, and operator surfaces unless a caller is explicitly using the operating-model lane.
surface_rows.why: The worker file already warns against resurrecting a second legacy workflow path. Believe it.

surface_rows.current_role: Canonical memory-graph mutation owner plus traversal and ranking substrate.
surface_rows.truth_boundary: Mutation authority belongs to the memory repository; traversal, ranking, and blast-radius logic belong to graph reasoning and read-side projection.
surface_rows.must_change: Do not promote graph reasoning code into a new cross-cutting write authority. Keep graph writes scoped to memory ownership only.
surface_rows.why: The repo already has a clean split available here. The mistake would be widening the graph into everybody's source of truth.

surface_rows.current_role: Context building, packet assembly, prompt authority resolution, execution dispatch, verification, and receipt writing support for workflow jobs.
surface_rows.truth_boundary: Supports execution; does not own workflow control truth, artifact truth, or event truth.
surface_rows.must_change: Keep it narrow. Do not invent a generic execution primitive above or beside workflow control and handoff authority.
surface_rows.why: These modules are harness glue around the real authorities. Making them their own top-level authority family would increase ambiguity.

why_these_survived: The repo's durable advantage is explicit authority plus inspectable state, not a larger number of primitives.
Handoff was the real missing seam, and it now exists; the remaining work is adoption and de-overlap.
Most other debates are boundary questions: which existing surface is truth, which surface is projection, and which surface should be narrowed or removed.
